result:
One autonomous improvement pass complete. I implemented the prior pass's flagged next step: a per-profile **Itinerarios** page that converts the `start` triage from "what you are" into "what to do next, guide by guide."

SUMMARY:
Added `content/docs/itinerarios.mdx` — five sequenced, phased reading routes (high-risk provider; high-risk deployer; public sector + EIDF/FRIA; transparency-only; minimal risk), where each step links the relevant guide and names the obligation/article and applicable deadline. Wired it into the information architecture so the diagnosis now lands on an actionable plan:
- `content/docs/meta.json` — `itinerarios` added right after `start` (2nd sidebar entry).
- `content/docs/start.mdx` — "Tu ruta según el resultado" cards re-pointed from single-guide/summary links to the matching itinerary anchors; added a public-sector card and a hub lead-in.
- `content/docs/index.mdx` — generic "Recorridos recomendados" upgraded to per-profile "Recorridos por perfil" cards.
- `content/docs/introduction.mdx` — new "Cómo recorrerlo" entry for itineraries.
- `content/docs/summary.mdx` — generic reading-order section now flags it as the provider path and points other profiles to the itineraries.
- `.hermes/overnight/progress.md` — Pasada 2 entry added.

VALIDATION:
- `npm run build` succeeded: TypeScript passes, 74 static pages generated (incl. `/docs/itinerarios`, its `llms.mdx` and OG image).
- Anchor integrity: all five `<h2>` ids in the generated HTML match the cross-link anchors exactly, including accented `administración-y-sector-público` and `riesgo-mínimo`.
- Link integrity: all 22 internal `/docs/...` links emitted by the new page resolve to generated HTML (0 broken); the one external link (EUR-Lex) reuses the URL already vetted in `official-sources.mdx`.
- Content grounded in already-vetted `summary.mdx`/`start.mdx`; Art. 26 (deployer obligations) and Art. 27 (FRIA) confirmed via web search against EUR-Lex sources. No fabricated data. No commit made (left for the overnight harness).

NEXT_IDEA:
Add a standardized "De un vistazo" header to each of the 16 guides (article, responsible role, itinerary phase, dependencies, when to use) sourced from the summary — so entering a guide from an itinerary reminds the reader where it fits and where to go next, closing the navigation loop. High-value alternative: enable Fumadocs static search (Orama) to make the ~2 MB corpus keyword-searchable.
